clusters.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
clusters.neutral_headline: Tense election night in Colombia as country heads to presidential runoff
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera, NPR News and others

items.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
items.standfirst: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksThe far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella won the first round of Colombia’s presidential election on Sunday and will face senator Iván Cepeda, the candidate backed by leftwing president Gustavo Petro, in the runoff.With 99.97% of ballots counted, the outsider and Donald Trump admirer Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014. Continue reading...
items.content: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksThe far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella won the first round of Colombia’s presidential election on Sunday and will face senator Iván Cepeda, the candidate backed by leftwing president Gustavo Petro, in the runoff.With 99.97% of ballots counted, the outsider and Donald Trump admirer Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014. Continue reading...

items.title: Tense election night in Colombia as country heads to presidential runoff
items.standfirst: Colombia’s election heads to a June 21 runoff after a tight first round between de la Espriella and Cepeda.
items.content: Colombia’s election heads to a June 21 runoff after a tight first round between de la Espriella and Cepeda.

items.title: Right-wing candidate pulls ahead in first round of Colombia's presidential vote
items.standfirst: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.
items.content: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.(Image credit: Fernando Vergara)

items.title: Colombia’s Petro sows doubt about election as favored successor heads to runoff against pro-Trump rival
items.standfirst: Colombia’s outgoing president sowed doubt Sunday about his country’s elections, which showed his preferred candidate, Iván Cepeda, headed to a runoff next month against right-wing opponent Abelardo de la Espriella
items.content: Colombia’s outgoing president sowed doubt Sunday about his country’s elections, which showed his preferred candidate, Iván Cepeda, headed to a runoff next month against right-wing opponent Abelardo de la Espriella

items.title: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival
items.standfirst: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.
items.content: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.

items.title: Colombia’s outsider candidate defied the polls
items.standfirst: Far-right outsider, Abelardo de la Espriella, will face leftist senator, Ivan Cepeda on June 21.
items.content: Far-right outsider, Abelardo de la Espriella, will face leftist senator, Ivan Cepeda on June 21.

items.title: Cepeda, de la Espriella advance in Colombia’s presidential election
items.standfirst: The left-wing senator and far-right newcomer will face each other in a run-off on June 21, with security a top issue.
items.content: The left-wing senator and far-right newcomer will face each other in a run-off on June 21, with security a top issue.

items.title: Colombian presidential election headed for runoff
items.standfirst: Far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella and far-left Ivan Cepeda are advancing to a runoff in Colombia's Presidential Election.
items.content: Far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella and far-left Ivan Cepeda are advancing to a runoff in Colombia's Presidential Election.

items.title: Colombia's presidential race goes to a runoff: De la Espriella vs. Cepeda
items.standfirst: Colombians voted Sunday in the first round of presidential elections, choosing between a reformist left seeking to retain power and a hard-line right promising security amid escalating violence by armed groups. With 99.99% of votes counted, far-right outsider Abelardo de la Espriella leads with more than 10 million votes (43.70%), followed by leftist Iván Cepeda with 9.6 million (40.93%). Amid mutual accusations, the two will face off in a runoff on June 21.
items.content: Colombians voted Sunday in the first round of presidential elections, choosing between a reformist left seeking to retain power and a hard-line right promising security amid escalating violence by armed groups. With 99.99% of votes counted, far-right outsider Abelardo de la Espriella leads with more than 10 million votes (43.70%), followed by leftist Iván Cepeda with 9.6 million (40.93%). Amid mutual accusations, the two will face off in a runoff on June 21.

items.title: Colombia goes to polls in election pitting outgoing leader’s ally against pro-Trump candidates
items.standfirst: Ballots are being cast in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential electionsColombians are casting ballots in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict.The vote on Sunday, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed a historic peace pact with guerrillas of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Continue reading...
items.content: Ballots are being cast in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential electionsColombians are casting ballots in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict.The vote on Sunday, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed a historic peace pact with guerrillas of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Continue reading...

items.title: Colombia prepares to go to polls in election shadowed by resurgence of political violence
items.standfirst: Sunday’s presidential vote is contest between left and right – and between contradictory proposals for dealing with the decades-long armed conflictMateo Pérez Rueda was one internship away from completing a degree in political science. The 24-year-old also worked as a bicycle delivery rider and sold fruit salads and juice to finance his passion: the Colombian independent digital magazine El Confidente.On 4 May he travelled to Briceño, in the western province of Antioquia, to report on the long-running conflict between the army, paramilitaries and dissidents of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Continue reading...
items.content: Sunday’s presidential vote is contest between left and right – and between contradictory proposals for dealing with the decades-long armed conflictMateo Pérez Rueda was one internship away from completing a degree in political science. The 24-year-old also worked as a bicycle delivery rider and sold fruit salads and juice to finance his passion: the Colombian independent digital magazine El Confidente.On 4 May he travelled to Briceño, in the western province of Antioquia, to report on the long-running conflict between the army, paramilitaries and dissidents of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). military said it carried out what it called self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites over the weekend, while Iran said it targeted an air base used in the U

items.title: US says it struck Iranian radar sites as Kuwait reports missile and drone attacks
items.standfirst: Iran and the US say they have carried out fresh attacks around the Strait of Hormuz, the third known escalation in a week.
items.content: Iran and the US say they have carried out fresh attacks around the Strait of Hormuz, the third known escalation in a week.

items.title: U.S. bombs Iranian military sites and Kuwait is hit by drone and missile fire
items.standfirst: The U.S. military says it bombed Iranian radar and drone control sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American MQ-1 Predator drone this weekend. Kuwait said its air defenses opened fire on Monday.
items.content: The U.S. military says it bombed Iranian radar and drone control sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American MQ-1 Predator drone this weekend. Kuwait said its air defenses opened fire on Monday. (Image credit: AP)

items.title: Iran targets American base in region after US strikes Iranian air defences
items.standfirst: Attacks reported in Kuwait – home to a US military base – and around the strait of Hormuz in latest exchange of fire as peace negotiations continueMiddle East live – latest updatesThe US said it struck Iranian military sites at the weekend and Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said on Monday it had targeted a US base in response, the latest in a series of exchanges amid negotiations to end the three-month-old war.The strikes on Iran’s Gulf coast were in response to “aggressive Iranian actions that included the shootdown of a US MQ-1 drone that was operating over international waters”, the US military’s central command (Centcom) said on X. Continue reading...
items.content: Attacks reported in Kuwait – home to a US military base – and around the strait of Hormuz in latest exchange of fire as peace negotiations continueMiddle East live – latest updatesThe US said it struck Iranian military sites at the weekend and Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said on Monday it had targeted a US base in response, the latest in a series of exchanges amid negotiations to end the three-month-old war.The strikes on Iran’s Gulf coast were in response to “aggressive Iranian actions that included the shootdown of a US MQ-1 drone that was operating over international waters”, the US military’s central command (Centcom) said on X. items.title: U.S. says it struck Iranian drone and radar sites, as Iran claims attack on air base
items.standfirst: The U.S. military said it carried out what it called self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites over the weekend, while Iran said it targeted an air base used in the U.S. attack as the two countries continued to trade strikes amid talks to end their war
items.content: The U.S. military said it carried out what it called self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites over the weekend, while Iran said it targeted an air base used in the U.S. attack as the two countries continued to trade strikes amid talks to end their war

items.title: Middle East live: US bombs Iranian military sites, Kuwait intercepts drone and missile attacks
items.standfirst: The US conducted strikes on military sites in Iran’s ‌Goruk and Qeshm Island over the weekend in response to “aggressive Iranian actions”, the military said late ‌Sunday. Meanwhile Kuwaiti air defences on Monday intercepted "hostile missile and drone attacks", its military said. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: The US conducted strikes on military sites in Iran’s ‌Goruk and Qeshm Island over the weekend in response to “aggressive Iranian actions”, the military said late ‌Sunday. Meanwhile Kuwaiti air defences on Monday intercepted "hostile missile and drone attacks", its military said. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

clusters.title: Violent clashes give way to peaceful PSG parade after Champions League win
clusters.neutral_headline: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
bullet_summary: The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops
Reported by NPR News, France24, BBC World and others

items.title: Violent clashes give way to peaceful PSG parade after Champions League win
items.standfirst: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.
items.content: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.(Image credit: Emma Da Silva)

items.title: PSG parade champions league trophy as they celebrate back-to-back titles
items.standfirst: Paris Saint-Germain brought the Champions League trophy back to France, taking it from the Champ-de-Mars to the Parc des Princes.
items.content: Paris Saint-Germain brought the Champions League trophy back to France, taking it from the Champ-de-Mars to the Parc des Princes.

items.title: PSG celebrate Champions League title with 48,000 fans at Parc des Princes stadium
items.standfirst: The European champions celebrated back-to-back titles with jubilant fans at their Paris home after a day of revelry in the French capital.
items.content: The European champions celebrated back-to-back titles with jubilant fans at their Paris home after a day of revelry in the French capital.

items.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
items.standfirst: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.
items.content: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.

items.title: Jubilant PSG fans celebrate Champions League victory by the Eiffel Tower
items.standfirst: A huge crowd of jubilant PSG supporters gathered near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate their team's second Champions League title victory. Thousands of people celebrated across France throughout the night but the parties were blighted by incidents, clashes with police, vandalism, a deadly road accident and hundreds of arrests, according to authorities.
items.content: A huge crowd of jubilant PSG supporters gathered near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate their team's second Champions League title victory. Thousands of people celebrated across France throughout the night but the parties were blighted by incidents, clashes with police, vandalism, a deadly road accident and hundreds of arrests, according to authorities.

items.title: Hundreds of people detained during celebrations of Paris Saint-German’s Champions League win
items.standfirst: French police detained 780 people across France as violent clashes in Paris and other cities erupted after Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League title.
items.content: French police detained 780 people across France as violent clashes in Paris and other cities erupted after Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League title.

items.title: In pictures: Paris hosts PSG victory parade after back-to-back Champions League titles
items.standfirst: Fresh from their Champions League final win in Budapest, Paris Saint-Germain players paraded their trophy on the Champs-de-Mars in front of the Eiffel Tower on Sunday, ahead of a presidential reception at the Élysée Palace and a party at their Parc des Princes stadium. But security was tight after overnight celebrations were marred by violence, a deadly road accident and hundreds of arrests.
items.content: Fresh from their Champions League final win in Budapest, Paris Saint-Germain players paraded their trophy on the Champs-de-Mars in front of the Eiffel Tower on Sunday, ahead of a presidential reception at the Élysée Palace and a party at their Parc des Princes stadium. But security was tight after overnight celebrations were marred by violence, a deadly road accident and hundreds of arrests.

items.title: Hundreds arrested and dozens of police injured after Champions League riots in France
items.standfirst: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.
items.content: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

items.title: French police arrest 780 after violent clashes as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win
items.standfirst: Interior minister says 57 officers injured as rioters set fires and vandalise shops in about 15 citiesFrench police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League.The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops. One small group even tried to storm a Paris police station. Continue reading...
items.content: Interior minister says 57 officers injured as rioters set fires and vandalise shops in about 15 citiesFrench police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League.The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops. One small group even tried to storm a Paris police station. Continue reading...

items.title: Hundreds arrested across France during unrest after PSG win - as more than 50 officers injured
Over 700 rioters detained across France after PSG's Champions League victory celebrations
items.standfirst: A second Champions League trophy for Paris Saint-Germain, but an evening marred by riots in the French capital and 15 other cities across the country. Groups of supporters clashed with the police, resulting in 780 arrests and 57 security forces injured. Details by Daniel Quinlan.
items.content: A second Champions League trophy for Paris Saint-Germain, but an evening marred by riots in the French capital and 15 other cities across the country. Groups of supporters clashed with the police, resulting in 780 arrests and 57 security forces injured. Details by Daniel Quinlan.

items.title: PSG fans await Champions League trophy celebration in Paris
items.standfirst: Paris Saint-Germain are the champions of Europe after their win on penalties against Arsenal last night. This afternoon, the team heads back to the French capital for a victory parade in the heart of the city. Supporters are already gathering around the Eiffel Tower to welcome them. Details by Emily Boyle.
items.content: Paris Saint-Germain are the champions of Europe after their win on penalties against Arsenal last night. This afternoon, the team heads back to the French capital for a victory parade in the heart of the city. Supporters are already gathering around the Eiffel Tower to welcome them. Details by Emily Boyle.

items.title: PSG players parade Champions League trophy after night of unrest in Paris
items.standfirst: Tens of thousands of jubilant supporters gathered at the Eiffel Tower and the Parc des Princes stadium on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's second successive Champions League title victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight across France and led police to detain hundreds of people.
items.content: Tens of thousands of jubilant supporters gathered at the Eiffel Tower and the Parc des Princes stadium on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's second successive Champions League title victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight across France and led police to detain hundreds of people.

clusters.title: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Sky News World, France24 and others

items.title: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.
items.content: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.

items.title: Israel seizes Crusades-era fortress in Lebanon as it expands offensive
items.standfirst: Israeli forces have captured a strategically important fortress from the Crusades, marking the country's deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter of a century.
items.content: Israeli forces have captured a strategically important fortress from the Crusades, marking the country's deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter of a century.

items.title: What is Lebanon's Beaufort Castle, and why have Israeli forces captured it?
items.standfirst: The Israeli army has taken control of Beaufort castle in Southern Lebanon, crossing over the Litani river, which they had been using as a de facto boundary. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called the operation a "dramatic shift" in the Israeli offensive in Lebanon. The Israeli army now has declared all land between the Litani and Zaharani River a conflict zone. Details by Caroline Baum.
items.content: The Israeli army has taken control of Beaufort castle in Southern Lebanon, crossing over the Litani river, which they had been using as a de facto boundary. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called the operation a "dramatic shift" in the Israeli offensive in Lebanon. The Israeli army now has declared all land between the Litani and Zaharani River a conflict zone. Details by Caroline Baum.

items.title: Netanyahu vows to expand Israel’s grip on Lebanon after deepest incursion in 26 years
items.standfirst: Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and captured a strategic mountain in Lebanon, marking the deepest incursion in 26 years.
items.content: Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and captured a strategic mountain in Lebanon, marking the deepest incursion in 26 years.

The Tagor was detained Sunday morning in international waters with the help of Britain and other partners, the French president said

items.title: French Navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in high seas, Macron says
items.standfirst: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.
items.content: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.

items.title: Macron says French navy has boarded Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic
items.standfirst: French president says it is ‘unacceptable for ships to circumvent international sanctions’, adding operation took place with support of UKThe French navy has boarded an oil tanker that was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, the French president, Emmanuel Macron, has said.Macron wrote on X: “This operation took place in the Atlantic Ocean, on the high seas, with the support of several partners, including the United Kingdom, in strict compliance with the law of the sea,. Continue reading...

items.title: France and allies intercept sanctioned Russian oil tanker in Atlantic
items.standfirst: France and its partners intercepted a Russian oil tanker circumventing sanctions in the Atlantic over the weekend, President Emmanuel Macron said in a social media post on Monday. The Tagor was detained Sunday morning in international waters with the help of Britain and other partners, the French president said.
items.content: France and its partners intercepted a Russian oil tanker circumventing sanctions in the Atlantic over the weekend, President Emmanuel Macron said in a social media post on Monday. The Tagor was detained Sunday morning in international waters with the help of Britain and other partners, the French president said.

items.title: Cory Booker has ‘concerns’ with Graham Platner’s Senate bid after leaked texts
items.standfirst: US senator says Platner, whose wife says he sent sexually explicit messages to other women, has ‘questions to answer’A high-profile Democrat has expressed concerns with party candidate Graham Platner’s Maine US Senate campaign amid revelations that Platner reportedly sent a number of sexually explicit messages to other women while married.“Yes, I have concerns,” Cory Booker, the US senator from New Jersey, said Sunday on ABC’s This Week when host Jonathan Karl when asked about the Platner revelations. “That guy has questions to answer – and that’s what campaigns are for.” Continue reading...
items.content: US senator says Platner, whose wife says he sent sexually explicit messages to other women, has ‘questions to answer’A high-profile Democrat has expressed concerns with party candidate Graham Platner’s Maine US Senate campaign amid revelations that Platner reportedly sent a number of sexually explicit messages to other women while married.“Yes, I have concerns,” Cory Booker, the US senator from New Jersey, said Sunday on ABC’s This Week when host Jonathan Karl when asked about the Platner revelations. “That guy has questions to answer – and that’s what campaigns are for.” Continue reading...

items.standfirst: The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.
items.content: The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.(Image credit: Julio Cortez)

items.title: United flight to Spain turns back midair after possible security threat
items.standfirst: Security came to inspect aircraft in Newark after report of Bluetooth device with a ‘certain four-letter word’A United Airlines plane bound for Spain from Newark Liberty international airport turned around mid-flight on Saturday due to a possible security threat.That came one day after another United Airlines flight bound for Minneapolis from Chicago was diverted to Madison, Wisconsin, on Friday because an unruly passenger evidently tried to breach the cockpit. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Five recover from Ebola - as Brazil investigates two suspected cases
clusters.neutral_headline: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
bullet_summary: Five people have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus, the head of the World Health Organisation said - as suspected cases were investigated in Brazil and Italy
Reported by Sky News World, Guardian World

items.title: Five recover from Ebola - as Brazil investigates two suspected cases

items.content: Five people have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus, the head of the World Health Organisation said - as suspected cases were investigated in Brazil and Italy.

items.title: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
items.standfirst: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri provinceContaining the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against stringent medical protocols for handling victims’ bodies. Continue reading...
items.content: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri provinceContaining the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against stringent medical protocols for handling victims’ bodies. items.title: WHO chief heads to Ebola epicentre - as Brazil investigates suspected case
items.standfirst: The World Health Organisation (WHO) chief has visited the province hardest hit by the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), urging residents to seek treatment and practice safe burials.
items.content: The World Health Organisation (WHO) chief has visited the province hardest hit by the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), urging residents to seek treatment and practice safe burials.

items.title: Israel seizes medieval castle as it expands major offensive in southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: Israel has captured the 12th-century Beaufort castle that overlooks southern Lebanon and northern Israel. The move suggests Israel is planning an extended military presence in Lebanon.
items.content: Israel has captured the 12th-century Beaufort castle that overlooks southern Lebanon and northern Israel. The move suggests Israel is planning an extended military presence in Lebanon.

clusters.title: Israel seizes strategic castle in deepest incursion into Lebanon in 26 years
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel seizes strategic castle in deepest incursion into Lebanon in 26 years
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, France24

items.title: Israel seizes strategic castle in deepest incursion into Lebanon in 26 years
items.standfirst: Experts say capture is largely symbolic, but it complicates efforts to extend the ceasefire between US and IranIsraeli troops have captured a clifftop castle as they made their deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than 26 years, further shattering a nominal US-brokered ceasefire and complicating efforts to extend the separate truce between Washington and Tehran.After days of intense fighting and airstrikes in nearby villages, the Israeli defence minister, Israel Katz, said the military had captured Beaufort Castle, also known as Qalaat al-Shaqif, which it had used as a base during its previous occupation of southern Lebanon between 1982 and 2000. Continue reading...
items.content: Experts say capture is largely symbolic, but it complicates efforts to extend the ceasefire between US and IranIsraeli troops have captured a clifftop castle as they made their deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than 26 years, further shattering a nominal US-brokered ceasefire and complicating efforts to extend the separate truce between Washington and Tehran.After days of intense fighting and airstrikes in nearby villages, the Israeli defence minister, Israel Katz, said the military had captured Beaufort Castle, also known as Qalaat al-Shaqif, which it had used as a base during its previous occupation of southern Lebanon between 1982 and 2000. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel seizes strategic Beaufort Castle in Lebanon, issues new evacuation orders
items.standfirst: Israeli troops have captured Beaufort castle in Lebanon, built in Crusader times – and a symbol of Israel's advancement deeper into Lebanese territory. Israeli forces have used it as a base during its two decade occupation of Lebanon. Israel has called on Lebanese civilians to evacuate yet more towns and villages in the south, as it pushes forward, saying it is fighting Hezbollah. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Jerusalem, Noga Tarnopolsky.
items.content: Israeli troops have captured Beaufort castle in Lebanon, built in Crusader times – and a symbol of Israel's advancement deeper into Lebanese territory. Israeli forces have used it as a base during its two decade occupation of Lebanon. Israel has called on Lebanese civilians to evacuate yet more towns and villages in the south, as it pushes forward, saying it is fighting Hezbollah. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Jerusalem, Noga Tarnopolsky.

items.title: Top W.H.O. official in Ebola epicenter as deadly outbreak spreads
items.standfirst: The head of the World Health Organization Saturday arrived in eastern DR Congo’s Bunia, a city at the heart of an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola, where the virus is still spreading faster than the response. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.
items.content: The head of the World Health Organization Saturday arrived in eastern DR Congo’s Bunia, a city at the heart of an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola, where the virus is still spreading faster than the response. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.

clusters.title: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
clusters.neutral_headline: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Sky News World

items.title: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
items.standfirst: Reggio Emilia prefect stops gig after Jewish community ‘concerns’ over rapper’s previous antisemitic remarksA Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled over “public order and safety issues”.The 48-year-old rapper, who changed his name to Ye in 2021, was due to perform at the Pulse of Gaia festival at the RCF Arena in Reggio Emilia on 18 July, but the city’s prefect, Salvatore Angieri, stopped the gigs after “concerns” from the local Jewish community over previous antisemitic remarks by West. Continue reading...
items.content: Reggio Emilia prefect stops gig after Jewish community ‘concerns’ over rapper’s previous antisemitic remarksA Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled over “public order and safety issues”.The 48-year-old rapper, who changed his name to Ye in 2021, was due to perform at the Pulse of Gaia festival at the RCF Arena in Reggio Emilia on 18 July, but the city’s prefect, Salvatore Angieri, stopped the gigs after “concerns” from the local Jewish community over previous antisemitic remarks by West. Continue reading...

items.content: A Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled over public order and security concerns.

clusters.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
clusters.neutral_headline: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, NBC World

items.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
items.standfirst: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find themFour more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them.The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
items.content: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find themFour more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them.The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...

items.title: Four more men rescued from flooded cave after becoming trapped while searching for gold
items.standfirst: Five men have now been freed after spending more than a week trapped in a flooded cave in Laos, according to rescuers, with two others still missing
items.content: Five men have now been freed after spending more than a week trapped in a flooded cave in Laos, according to rescuers, with two others still missing
